_phase2_gate_summary.notes: Sprint 6's perform_upsert() hook block is code-present but flag-OFF (ASSOC_SIMILARITY_WRITE_ENABLED=False). Observed p95 (0.848 ms) is 13.6% BELOW the Sprint 5 baseline (0.981 ms), well within the <=10% regression gate. Sub-millisecond measurements have material run-to-run variance (stdev ~0.2 ms across 30 samples); a 5-trial sweep at this iteration count saw p95 values of 0.768, 0.503, 0.917, 1.415, 0.796 ms with a median of ~0.796 ms — consistent with the canonical single run captured here and below the Sprint 5 baseline. Structurally, the flag-off hook path is a single `if` condition and a branch-not-taken: no imports, no allocations, no object construction. The delta is within measurement noise and the gate is comfortably met.
_phase2_gate_summary.measurement_methodology: Same harness (measure_perform_upsert_latency.py) and same fixture (fx-01-decision-novatrade) as Sprint 5, so the comparison is apples-to-apples. 30 iterations with 3 warmup.
_phase2_gate_summary.hook_code_path_when_flag_off: Single attribute read (settings.ASSOC_SIMILARITY_WRITE_ENABLED), single boolean AND against `success`, branch-not-taken. No other work.
_phase2_gate_summary.hook_code_path_when_flag_on_first_call: Lazy import + construct SimilarityLinker once, then enqueue_link() which schedules a background task and returns. Subsequent calls skip the lazy path.
_phase2_gate_summary.next_action: Phase 2 Sprint 6 code changes are latency-safe. Promote to review.

phase2_gate: Phase 2 p95 overhead must not regress by more than 10% over this baseline
notes: perform_upsert() under ASSOC_* all-False with every backend mocked; isolates orchestration cost. Hook block from Sprint 6 is code-present but flag-off.
